pemPublicPlaceholder: Paste PEM public key (SPKI: -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----)
pemPrivatePlaceholder: Paste PEM private key (PKCS8: -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----)
pemHint: Only PKCS8 / SPKI accepted. Convert with: openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -nocrypt -in old.pem -out pkcs8.pem

errors.invalidPemFormat: Unrecognized PEM format. Expected -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY----- (PKCS8) or -----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY----- (SPKI).
errors.pkcs1NotSupported: PKCS1 keys are not supported. Convert with: openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -nocrypt -in pkcs1.pem -out pkcs8.pem  (private)  /  openssl rsa -in pkcs1.pem -RSAPublicKey_in -pubout -out spki.pem  (public)
errors.sec1NotSupported: SEC1 EC keys are not supported. Convert with: openssl pkcs8 -topk8 -nocrypt -in sec1.pem -out pkcs8.pem
errors.encryptedKeyNotSupported: Encrypted PEM keys are not supported. Decrypt first with: openssl pkcs8 -in encrypted.pem -out plain.pem
errors.certNotSupported: Paste a public key, not a certificate. Extract with: openssl x509 -in cert.pem -pubkey -noout
errors.expectedPublic: Verify needs a public key (-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----), not a private key
errors.expectedPrivate: Sign needs a private key (-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----), not a public key
errors.algNoneRejected: alg=none is rejected — this token is not signed and verification is not meaningful

descriptions.whatIs: JWT (JSON Web Token, RFC 7519) is an open standard for securely transmitting information between parties as a compact JSON object. It is commonly used for authentication and authorization.
descriptions.structure: A JWT consists of three Base64URL-encoded parts separated by dots: Header.Payload.Signature. The header specifies the token type and signing algorithm. The payload contains the claims. The signature verifies integrity.
descriptions.algorithms: HMAC (HS256/384/512), RSA (RS256/384/512), RSA-PSS (PS256/384/512), and ECDSA (ES256/384/512). The unsigned `alg=none` is rejected for safety.
descriptions.keyFormats: HMAC: any UTF-8 string. Asymmetric: PEM only — PKCS8 (-----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----) for signing, SPKI (-----BEGIN PUBLIC KEY-----) for verifying. PKCS1, SEC1, encrypted PEM, and X.509 certificates are not accepted; the tool will tell you the exact openssl command to convert them.
